vehicle_classes.name: Saloon Cars and Taxis
vehicle_classes.description: Passenger cars, saloon vehicles, and taxis including sedans, hatchbacks, station wagons, and SUVs used for private or hire transport.

vehicle_classes.name: Light Goods, Vans, Pickups and 4WD
vehicle_classes.description: Light commercial vehicles including vans, pickups (single and double cab), and four-wheel-drive utility vehicles. Gross Vehicle Weight typically under 3.5 tonnes.

vehicle_classes.name: Small Bus / Minibuses and Matatus
vehicle_classes.description: Minibuses (14–18 seats) commonly known as Matatus or taxis in Uganda. Primary mode of public transport on most national roads.

vehicle_classes.name: Medium Bus - Coasters
vehicle_classes.description: Medium-sized buses (25–35 seats), commonly Toyota Coasters. Used for intercity and semi-urban public transport.

vehicle_classes.name: Large Buses
vehicle_classes.description: Full-size passenger buses (45+ seats) including intercity coaches and urban transit buses. Two or three axle configurations.
vehicle_classes.axle_config: 1-2 (2 axles, rear dual tyres) or 1-1-2 (3 axles)

vehicle_classes.name: Light Single Unit Trucks - Dynas and Tractors
vehicle_classes.description: Light single-unit trucks (2 axles), typically Toyota Dynas and Isuzu NPRs. Used for local goods distribution and agriculture. GVW 3.5–7.5 tonnes.

vehicle_classes.name: Medium/Large Single Unit Trucks (2-4 Axles, 7+ Ton)
vehicle_classes.description: Medium and large rigid trucks with 2 to 4 axles. Includes tipper trucks, tankers, and heavy rigid freight vehicles. GVW exceeds 7 tonnes.

vehicle_classes.name: Truck Trailer and Semi-Trailer (5+ Axles)
vehicle_classes.description: Articulated vehicles with tractor unit and semi-trailer, or rigid truck with drawbar trailer. Total of 5 or more axles. Dominant mode for long-haul freight on Northern Corridor (Mombasa–Kampala–Juba/Kigali).

vehicle_classes.name: Motorcycles and Scooters
vehicle_classes.description: Motorized two-wheelers including boda-bodas (motorcycle taxis), personal motorcycles, and scooters. Dominant mode of transport by vehicle count across Uganda.

vehicle_classes.name: Bicycles
vehicle_classes.description: Non-motorised transport — pedal-powered bicycles used for personal transport and goods carriage.

vehicle_classes.name: Carts
vehicle_classes.description: Animal-drawn or human-powered carts used for goods transport, particularly in rural areas.

axle_load_limits.title: Schedule 4 — Permissible Axle Loads (Uganda National Roads Authority Vehicle Dimensions and Load Control Regulations, 2017)

limits.description: Single axle with 2 conventional tyres (steering axle)
Single axle with 4 conventional tyres (dual rear)
Tandem axle group with 8 conventional tyres
Tandem steering axle group with 4 conventional tyres
Tandem axle group with 4 super-single tyres
Tridem axle group with 12 conventional tyres
Tridem axle group with 6 super-single tyres
Liftable single axle with 4 conventional tyres
Liftable single axle with 2 super-single tyres

vehicle_dimensions.title: Maximum Vehicle Dimensions (Uganda National Roads Authority Regulations, 2017)

encoder_mapping.description: Maps the ML model's LabelEncoder vehicle_class values to the classification IDs above
